description: Complete professional rewrite of README.md (Chinese) with cleaner structure, no redundancy, accurate feature documentation, and professional formatting. Also update pyproject.toml metadata for v5.5.
steps: Read the current README.md (1130 lines) and identify all redundant/repeated sections

Read src/augur/cli.py to confirm all 15 CLI commands are documented correctly (analyze, consensus, list-personas, mcp-server, api, inject-soul, telegram, slack, wechat, lark, cron-run, cron-start, watchlist-add, watchlist-show, backtest, ic-report, fetch)
Read src/augur/mcp_server.py to confirm all 6 MCP tools are documented
Read dashboard/app.py to confirm all 7 dashboard pages and REST API endpoints

Rewrite README.md from scratch with professional structure: hero section with version badge (v5.5), concise 3-line quickstart, feature matrix, 17 personas table, quick start guide, platform integration (consolidated - not 4 separate long sections), architecture overview (referencing SVG), version log (consolidated), contribution guide

Remove redundancy: current README has the same CLI commands listed in 3 places, platform info repeated in both dedicated sections and integration section. Consolidate to single authoritative section each.
Add bilingual header linking to README_EN.md: '[English](README_EN.md) | 中文'
Update pyproject.toml: bump version to 5.5.0, update description to be more professional, add project.urls (Homepage, Repository, Documentation), add keywords, add classifiers for PyPI
acceptance_criteria: README.md is professional, well-structured, under 800 lines (current is 1130 with lots of redundancy)
All 15+ CLI commands are accurately documented
All 4 platform bots (Telegram/Slack/WeChat/Lark) covered in a consolidated section
verification: python -c "import augur; print(augur.__version__)" prints the expected version
